steps.instruction: Clean and dry the wound thoroughly. Stop bleeding first.
steps.helpDetail: Glue will not stick to wet/bloody skin. It seals bacteria in, so cleaning is vital. Do not use on jagged or deep wounds.
Use fingers to approximate edges. Do not let edges roll inward.

steps.instruction: Apply thin layer of glue ON TOP of the crack, not inside.
steps.helpDetail: Do not get glue inside the wound—it prevents healing. Bridge the gap on the surface.

steps.instruction: Hold shut for 60 seconds until dry.
steps.helpDetail: Add a second layer if needed. Sloughs off naturally in 5-10 days.

steps.instruction: Verify impossibility of rescue. Tourniquet base of finger.
steps.helpDetail: Only if life is at risk (fire, rising water) and digit is irretrievably pinned. Tight constriction at base.

steps.instruction: Cut skin and tendons around the bone (circumferential).
steps.helpDetail: Cut down to the bone all the way around.

steps.instruction: Locate the joint (knuckle). Bend finger to open joint gap.
steps.helpDetail: Cut through the ligaments connecting the joint. Do not try to saw through bone.
Leave tourniquet on for max 2 hours if evacuating. Bandage stump heavily.
